---
title: "Session 14 · GLM Framework & Ordinal Models"
---

## Overview

Linear and logistic regression have felt like separate tools; this session reveals them as two members of one family. The Generalized Linear Model unifies them through a latent-response view, a link function, and a choice of distribution --- so that "ordinary" regression, logistic regression, and more all become special cases of a single template. We then extend the framework to ordered categorical outcomes via the proportional-odds model, reaching the "general" peak of the course's specific → general arc.

## Learning objectives

By the end of this session, you will be able to:

- State the three components of a Generalized Linear Model: random, systematic, and link.
- Use the latent-response view to motivate logistic and related link functions.
- Recognize linear and logistic regression as special cases of the GLM template.
- Fit a proportional-odds model for an ordinal outcome and interpret its cumulative log-odds.
- Select an appropriate GLM family and link for a given response type.

## This session's materials

- {{< fa book-open-reader >}} **Read:** [Chapter 12: Latent Response Models & GLMs](/notes/chapters/12-latent-response-glm.html){target="_blank"}
- {{< fa book-open-reader >}} **Read:** [Chapter 13: Models for Ordinal Responses](/notes/chapters/13-ordinal-models.html){target="_blank"}
- {{< fa laptop-code >}} **Lab:** [Lab 12: GLM Extensions](/labs/labs/lab12-logistic-advanced.html){target="_blank"}
- {{< fa laptop-code >}} **Lab:** [Lab 13: Ordinal Outcomes](/labs/labs/lab13-ordinal-outcomes.html){target="_blank"}
- {{< fa clipboard-list >}} **Project:** [Project 2 — Draft due](/assignment/project-2-draft.html)

## Key concepts

| Concept | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| **Generalized Linear Model** | A unifying framework relating a response to predictors through a random component, a linear predictor, and a link function. |
| **Link function** | The transformation connecting the mean of the response to the linear predictor. |
| **Exponential family** | The class of distributions (Normal, binomial, Poisson, and others) on which GLMs are built. |
| **Latent-response view** | An interpretation in which an observed category arises from thresholding an unobserved continuous variable. |
| **Linear predictor** | The weighted sum of predictors that the link function maps to the response's mean. |
| **Ordinal response** | An outcome with ordered categories but no assumed equal spacing between them. |
| **Proportional-odds model** | An ordinal GLM that models cumulative log-odds with a common slope across category thresholds. |
| **Special case** | A familiar model (e.g., linear or logistic regression) recovered by fixing the GLM's family and link. |
